items.headline: City updates youth services plans and library facilities strategy
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. The city also shared updates on library facilities improvements and customer experience enhancements.

items.headline: Zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A development project at 333 Franklin Street that was set to be reviewed will not be heard until a new date is announced.

items.headline: Grant awarded to Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza, expanding cultural programming in the city.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with new contract
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to its consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating continued work on this intersection safety improvement.

items.headline: Street pavement repair contracts awarded for 2026
items.summary: The city council approved bids and awarded contracts for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, addressing neighborhood street conditions.

items.headline: Active Transportation Plan updated with new project scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including changes to how projects are scored and evaluated. This plan affects future bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and other non-car transportation infrastructure across the city.

items.headline: Council votes to amend vacancy ordinance and reduce positions
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled and reduce the number of positions. This could affect future council composition and how successor candidates are selected or appointed.

items.headline: Senior housing complex with 65 units breaks ground on E St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a major 65-unit senior apartment complex valued at $8.8M on E St John St, significantly expanding affordable housing options for San José's aging population. This is one of the larger residential projects in the recent permit batch.
